complaint_text: I’m constantly tense in my shoulders and jaw. I don’t even notice until I get a headache. It feels like I’m bracing for something bad all the time, even on days that are supposed to be chill.
decomp_notes: Variables describe current, changeable physiological, cognitive and behavioral states directly implied by the complaint; granularity chosen to separate muscle-level tension, awareness, headache occurrence, anticipatory threat expectation, inability to relax on low-stress days, and habitual bracing behavior.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_label: Elevated resting muscle tension in shoulders and jaw
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Frequent, sustained increase in muscle tension in the shoulder and jaw regions during waking hours measurable by self-report (most days) or by observable tightening/posture.
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: 'I’m constantly tense in my shoulders and jaw.'
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Frequent, sustained increase in muscle tension in the shoulder and jaw regions during waking hours measurable by self-report (most days) or by observable tightening/posture.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_label: Low moment-to-moment awareness of bodily tension
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Delayed or limited interoceptive awareness such that the person does not notice increased muscle tension until a secondary symptom (headache) occurs.
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: 'I don’t even notice until I get a headache.'
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Delayed or limited interoceptive awareness such that the person does not notice increased muscle tension until a secondary symptom (headache) occurs.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Recurrent headaches that consistently follow or coincide with episodes of elevated shoulder/jaw tension and serve as the primary signal the tension is present.
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: 'I don’t even notice until I get a headache.'
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Recurrent headaches that consistently follow or coincide with episodes of elevated shoulder/jaw tension and serve as the primary signal the tension is present.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Frequent subjective expectation or sense that something bad is about to happen, experienced across days and contexts, producing ongoing mental tension.
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: 'It feels like I’m bracing for something bad all the time.'
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Frequent subjective expectation or sense that something bad is about to happen, experienced across days and contexts, producing ongoing mental tension.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Inability to downregulate physiological or psychological arousal even when the environment or schedule is objectively low-stress or 'chill'.
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: 'even on days that are supposed to be chill.'
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Inability to downregulate physiological or psychological arousal even when the environment or schedule is objectively low-stress or 'chill'.

criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_label: Habitual anticipatory muscle bracing in absence of immediate threat
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_criterion: Automatic activation of postural or facial muscles (bracing/tensing) as a learned or habitual response, occurring even when no immediate external threat is present.
criteria_operationalization_rows.variable_evidence: 'bracing for something bad all the time' (implying muscle bracing despite lack of immediate threat)
criteria_operationalization_rows.query_text_used: Automatic activation of postural or facial muscles (bracing/tensing) as a learned or habitual response, occurring even when no immediate external threat is present.

hyde_based_predictor_rankings.hyde_llm_summary: The case portrays chronic baseline muscle tension (shoulders/jaw), low interoceptive awareness, tension-linked headaches, persistent anticipatory threat, difficulty relaxing even on low-stress days, and habitual bracing. Optimal intervention here leverages a coordinated BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL strategy that (1) reduces peripheral muscle tension and arousal (bio), (2) enhances early tension detection and adaptive cognitive regulation (psycho/bio), and (3) broadens social-environmental supports and ergonomic context to sustain change. Levers are designed as modifiable predictors, not symptom labels, with clear measurement, feasible implementation, and targeted impact on the specified criteria (C01–C06). An integrated mix of relaxation training, mindfulness-based interoception, sleep/ergonomics optimization, cognitive reappraisal, habit change, and social support forms a scalable, low-risk plan that can be tracked via simple daily diaries and brief scales.
hyde_based_predictor_rankings.hyde_solutions_compact: S01:Daily progressive muscle relaxation (PMR) routine focusing on shoulders, jaw, and neck with 20 minutes twice daily | S02:Interoceptive awareness enhancement with brief mindfulness and body-scan | S03:Sleep optimization and circadian alignment | S04:Structured social support engagement (weekly check-ins with supportive others) | S05:Hourly micro-breaks with gentle movement and diaphragmatic breathing | S06:Threat reappraisal and cognitive reframing training | S07:Ergonomic and postural optimization with cue-based reminders | S08:Hydration optimization and caffeine intake management

guidance.framework_context: The output of this script is part of a larger framework that will ultimately construct a criterion+predictor model that is ready for data collection (e.g., via ecological momentary assessments, digital phenotyping, etc.).The general treatment algorithm is based on breadth-first search algorithm that first explores the secondary nodes (i.e., child nodes of the primary nodes BIO PSYCHO and SOCIAL).After data collection, the most relevant solution-predictors are re-selected for further data-analyes where then I focus on their subsequent child nodes).This algorithm goes on until user reaches salutogenic satisfactory mental state.
guidance.goal: Construct an initial criterion+predictor observation model ready for data collection and suitable for later gVAR analysis. Preserve complaint resolution while remaining estimable.

requested_variable_counts.note: If 'choice', choose an optimal number for gVAR feasibility while preserving coverage. Hard cap: total nodes <= 18 (avoid dense grid truncation).

measurement_principles: Prefer 1–9 Likert (anchored) for many constructs to increase variance.
Avoid pure binary nodes; if needed, measure intensity 1–9 + optional event flag (not as node).
Use 'since last prompt' for momentary EMA; 'morning diary' for last-night sleep; 'end_of_day' for daily summaries.
If passive data used, specify precise signal + aggregation window.
gvar_constraints: Coherent design: prompts/day must match per-variable sampling_per_day for momentary variables.
Avoid redundancy/collinearity (pick one broad construct rather than near-duplicates).
Prefer variables expected to fluctuate within-person at chosen timescale.
Provide plausible lag bins aligned with schedule: '0-6h', '0-24h', '24-48h', 'multi_lag'.
pairwise_estimation_requirement: You MUST output three dense relevance arrays: FULL P->C, FULL directed P->P (i!=j), FULL directed C->C (i!=j).
Dense score format: comma decimal separator and exactly 5 decimals.
Dense relevance scores are NOMOTHETIC plausibility of a directed relation at the chosen timescale (for gVAR adjacency), not diagnosis.
Absolute calibration anchors (be consistent): 0.00–0.10 none; 0.10–0.30 weak; 0.30–0.55 moderate; 0.55–0.80 strong; 0.80–1.00 very strong.
Relative constraint: within each fixed TARGET node, the ordering of relevance MUST reflect your best belief AND provided ranking signals.
Consistency constraint: each sparse edge copies the exact dense score into estimated_relevance_0_1 (numeric float) to 5 decimals.
Sparsity default: per TARGET include 2–4 strongest incoming edges (P->C), 1–3 (P->P, C->C).
Relevance tiers: very_low<0.10, low<0.30, medium<0.55, high<0.80, very_high>=0.80.
HARD CAP to avoid token truncation: keep total nodes <= 18 (e.g., <=8 criteria and <=10 predictors). Prefer fewer high-signal constructs.
sparse_edges_requirement: You MUST output edges, edges_pp, edges_cc.
Each edge must include expected_sign, relation_interpretation, lag_spec, estimated_relevance_0_1 (NUMBER), tier, notes.
CRITICAL: estimated_relevance_0_1 must match dense grid cell value to 5 decimals.
Choose sparse edges to maximize coverage and minimize redundancy; avoid lower-scoring inclusions vs omitted higher-scoring candidates for same target.
safety_and_scope: If acute risk is suggested, produce strong safety_notes encouraging professional help.
guidance.output_format: STRICT JSON matching the provided JSON Schema exactly.
